name: Tomatoes, Introduction
recipeIngredient: Quality: Select only disease-free, preferably vine-ripened, firm fruit for canning.
recipeInstructions:
1. Do not can tomatoes from dead or frost-killed vines.
2. Green tomatoes are more acidic than ripened fruit and can be canned safely using any of the following recommendations.
3. Acidification: To ensure safe acidity in whole, crushed, or juiced tomatoes, add two tablespoons of bottled lemon juice or 1/2 teaspoon of citric acid per quart of tomatoes. For pints, use one tablespoon bottled lemon juice or 1/4 teaspoon citric acid. Acid can be added directly to the jars before filling with product.
4. Add sugar to offset any acid taste if desired.
5. Four tablespoons of a 5 percent acidity vinegar per quart may be used instead of lemon juice or citric acid. However, vinegar may cause undesirable flavor changes.
6. Use a pressure canner for higher quality and more nutritious canned tomato products. If your pressure canner cannot operate above 15 PSI, select a process time at a lower pressure.
